mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of INDPRO monthly changes vs 60-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 6.0% in a 3-month window, it indicates the labor market is tightening, which historically leads to higher wage growth and consumer spending, supporting positive equity returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% in a 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: If after activation of the trigger, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not positive with statistical significance (t-test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous upward trends in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm broad economic expansion, which directly boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combined trend (principal component of INDPRO and RSAFS) does not show positive correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns exceeding 0.1 over rolling 3-year windows, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates compress valuation multiples as discount rates increase, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during Fed tightening cycles (defined as periods with at least two consecutive rate hikes), the average 20-day forward SPX return is not negative or is less negative than during non-tightening periods, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals labor market weakness, triggering short-term risk-off sentiment that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day SPX return after negative PAYEMS months is not negative or has hit rate below 50%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) indicates inflationary pressure that prompts expectations of monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations and leads to negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 12-month change in CPIAUCSL does not have a negative rank IC with 20-day forward SPX returns below 0.0 over any 10-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the year-over-year change in the CPI (negative real rates), equities benefit from low discount rates and inflation hedging demand, producing positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during periods with FEDFUNDS less than CPIAUCSL YoY change, 20-day forward SPX returns are not on average positive and statistically significant (t-test p>0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 500,000 units per month signals a severe housing downturn that weakens consumer wealth and construction activity, forecasting negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500,000 units in a month
failure_condition: If the average 60-day SPX return after triggers is not negative or hit rate is below 55%, reject.
